name: Italian Rum Cake
recipeInstructions:
The Cake
1. Beat the egg yolks, add water and 3/4 cup sugar; beat until thick.
2. Add vanilla, flour, and baking powder; mix well.
3. Clean the mixer blades and beat the egg whites until frothy.
4. Gradually add the remaining sugar to the whites and beat until stiff but not dry.
5. Gently fold the meringue into the yolk mixture.
6. Bake in a spring‑form pan for 50–60 minutes at 325 °F (165 °C).
7. In a saucepan, bring water and sugar to a boil; cook for 3–4 minutes.
8. Cool the syrup, then add rum and return it to heat briefly.
9. When the cake is completely cool, split into three layers.
Make the Filling
1. In a bowl, beat the egg yolks, cornstarch mixture (sugar, salt), and vanilla until smooth.
2. Strain the milk into a saucepan, add butter, and scald over medium heat.
3. Gradually pour the hot milk back into the yolk mixture while whisking constantly.
4. Cook for several minutes on low heat until thickened and glossy; remove from heat.
5. Stir in the chocolate and set aside to cool slightly.
6. Divide the filling in half; add more vanilla to one portion.
Assembly
1. Spread a thin layer of syrup over the bottom cake layer, then apply the vanilla‑infused filling.
2. Place the second layer on top, drizzle with additional syrup, and spread the chocolate‑enhanced filling.
3. Put the third layer on top and cover with the remaining syrup.
4. Frost with whipped cream and garnish with fresh strawberries.
This version maintains the original recipe's title, ingredients, and instructions while ensuring clarity and consistency in presentation.
